name: raindrop-io
description: Manage Raindrop.io bookmarks with AI assistance. Save and organize bookmarks, search your collection, manage reading lists, and organize research materials. Use when working with bookmarks, web research, reading lists, or when user mentions Raindrop.io.
qualityReason: Useful for bookmark automation and research
capabilities: Save bookmarks to Raindrop.io collections with automatic tagging
Search bookmarks by tags, collection, domain, date range, or full-text search
Manage reading lists with progress tracking and highlights
Organize research projects with collections, tags, and bulk operations
Create, update, and delete collections and tags
Extract and export highlights from saved articles
author: DanSega1
securitySummary: This is a legitimate instruction-only skill for managing Raindrop.io bookmarks via the official MCP server. All 356 static findings are false positives - they detect example commands and URLs in documentation files (README.md, references/*.md), not actual executable code. The skill contains no scripts, no malicious patterns, and no prompt injection attempts. The 'external_commands' detections are documentation showing users what commands they can type. The 'network' detections are legitimate documentation links. The 'blocker' detections are false positives from documentation text mentioning OAuth and API keys. Risk level: SAFE.
valueStatement: Save, organize, and search your Raindrop.io bookmarks through natural language conversations with AI. Perfect for researchers, developers, and anyone who wants to manage their web bookmarks efficiently.
limitations: Requires Raindrop.io Pro subscription ($28/year) for MCP access
Depends on official Raindrop.io MCP server which is in beta
Cannot access Raindrop.io without proper OAuth authorization
Some MCP features may have limited functionality during beta

useCases.title: Research Project Management
useCases.description: Create collections for research projects, systematically tag sources, and export highlights for literature reviews.

useCases.title: Developer Documentation Library
useCases.description: Save and organize technical documentation, API references, and code tutorials with systematic tagging.

useCases.targetUser: Anyone who wants to manage their reading
useCases.title: Personal Reading List
useCases.description: Save articles to a reading list, track progress, mark as read, and extract key highlights.

promptTemplates.title: Save a Bookmark
promptTemplates.prompt: Save this URL to my [collection name] collection: [URL]. Tag it with [tag1, tag2].
promptTemplates.scenario: Adding a new bookmark with specific collection and tags

promptTemplates.title: Search Bookmarks
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all my bookmarks about [topic] from the last [time period]. Show me results tagged with [tag].
promptTemplates.scenario: Finding bookmarks by topic and time range

promptTemplates.title: Manage Reading List
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me my reading list. Mark the bookmark at [URL] as read and add this highlight: [quote].
promptTemplates.scenario: Updating reading progress and adding highlights

promptTemplates.title: Organize Research
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new collection called [project name] for my [topic] research. Set up tags for [tag1, tag2, tag3].

bestPractices: Use consistent, lowercase hyphenated tags (e.g., 'machine-learning' not 'Machine Learning')
Create dedicated collections for different projects or topics to keep bookmarks organized
Review and clean up untagged bookmarks weekly to maintain a useful library
antiPatterns: Saving URLs without adding relevant tags or assigning to a collection
Using inconsistent tagging conventions across different bookmarks
Creating too many collections which makes it harder to find bookmarks

faq.question: Do I need a Raindrop.io subscription?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill requires a Raindrop.io Pro subscription ($28/year) because the official MCP server is only available to Pro users.

faq.question: Which AI clients work with this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Claude.ai, ChatGPT (Plus/Pro/Business), Cursor, Windsurf, and any other MCP-compatible client.

faq.question: Is my data secure?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill uses OAuth 2.1 for authentication. Your bookmarks remain in your Raindrop.io account and are not stored outside their servers.

faq.question: Can I use this skill offline?
faq.answer: No, this skill requires an internet connection to communicate with the Raindrop.io MCP server and API.

faq.question: What happens if the MCP server is down?
faq.answer: If the official Raindrop.io MCP server is unavailable, the skill won't be able to perform operations. Check status at help.raindrop.io/mcp for updates.

faq.question: Can I contribute to this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, this is an open-source skill. You can contribute by reporting issues, improving documentation, or sharing workflow patterns on GitHub.
